briefdescription.para: Iterates over each node in a red-black tree in ascending order.

detaileddescription.para: It is safe to remove the current node during the iteration.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the first node.
the name of the pointer to the nodes. This variable is declared in the scope of the loop.

memberdef.name: rbnode_foreach__
memberdef.initializer: for (struct rbnode *node = (first), \ *_rbnode_next_##n = (node) ? rbnode_next(node) : NULL; \ (node); (node) = _rbnode_next_##n, \ _rbnode_next_##n = (node) ? rbnode_next(node) : NULL)

briefdescription.para: Iterates over each node in a red-black tree in ascending order.

memberdef.definition: typedef int rbtree_cmp_t(const void *, const void *)
memberdef.name: rbtree_cmp_t

briefdescription.para: The type of a comparison function suitable for use in a red-black tree.

detaileddescription.para: p1 and p2 MUST be NULL or point to objects of the same type.

simplesect.para: an integer greater than, equal to, or less than 0 if the object at p1 is greater than, equal to, or less than the object at p2.

briefdescription.para: Initializes a node in a red-black tree.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the node to be initialized.
a pointer to the key for this node. The key MUST NOT be modified while the node is part of a tree.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the previous (in-order) node in a red-black tree with respect to node.

detaileddescription.para: This is, at worst, an O(log(n)) operation.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the next (in-order) node in a red-black tree with respect to node.

detaileddescription.para: This is, at worst, an O(log(n)) operation. However, visiting all nodes in order is an O(n) operation, and therefore, on average, O(1) for each node.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the tree to be initialized.
a pointer to the function used to compare two keys.

briefdescription.para: Returns true if the red-black tree is empty, and false if not.
Returns the size (in number of nodes) of a red-black tree.
Inserts a node into a red-black tree.

detaileddescription.para: This is an O(log(n)) operation. This function does not check whether a node with the same key already exists, or whether the node is already part of another tree.

briefdescription.para: Removes a node from a red-black tree.
Checks if a node is part of a red-black tree.

simplesect.para: true if the node was found in the tree, and false if not.

briefdescription.para: Finds a node in a red-black tree.

simplesect.para: a pointer to the node if found, or NULL if not.

briefdescription.para: Returns a pointer to the first (leftmost) node in a red-black tree.
Returns a pointer to the last (rightmost) node in a red-black tree.
Returns a pointer to the root node in a red-black tree.
This header file is part of the utilities library; it contains the red-black tree declarations.

detaileddescription.para: A red-black tree is a type of self-balancing binary tree. This implementation is based on chapters 12 and 13 in: T. H. Cormen et al., Introduction to Algorithms (third edition), MIT Press (2009).

The red-black tree implemented here is generic and can be used for any kind of key-value pair; only (void) pointers to keys are stored. Upon initialization of the tree, the user is responsible for providing a suitable comparison function (rbtree_cmp_t).
